{"id":"project-LICHEN-sjkp","title":"DIO grounded flag and MOP not validated before joining DODAG","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c:270-304

When processing a DIO and joining a DODAG, the code does not validate:
1. The grounded flag (RFC 6550 Section 6.3.1) - whether the DODAG has a route to a destination
2. The mode of operation (MOP) - whether the DODAG uses non-storing mode as expected

The DIO is parsed and these fields are available in the struct:
```c
dio->grounded = (gmop >> 7) & 1;
dio->mode_of_operation = (gmop >> 3) & 0x7;
```

But `lichen_rpl_dodag_process_dio` does not check them before calling `adopt_version()`.

Impact:
- Joining an ungrounded DODAG may result in no actual connectivity to root
- Joining a storing-mode DODAG when the code expects non-storing mode could cause routing failures

Suggested fix: Add MOP validation (reject if MOP != expected) and optionally warn/log on ungrounded DODAGs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:54:50.735042Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:54:50.735081Z"}
